name: Twilight Sparkle, Princess of Friendship
description:
Princess Twilight Sparkle

Princess Twilight Sparkle is the central protagonist of My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic .  A studious violet unicorn (later an alicorn), she is Celestia’s prized pupil in the magic of friendship.  As the series begins, Celestia sends her from Canterlot to Ponyville (accompanied by her dragon assistant Spike) to oversee Ponyville’s festivities and “make some friends”  .  In Ponyville Twilight bonds with the other five main ponies (Applejack, Rarity, Fluttershy, Rainbow Dash, and Pinkie Pie) – the Mane Six – discovering the Elements of Harmony and defeating Nightmare Moon.  This event convinces Celestia to let her remain in Ponyville to study friendship .  Over the course of Seasons 1–3, Twilight grows from an anxious, bookish loner into a confident leader of her friends.  In the Season 3 finale “Magical Mystery Cure” she uses friendship-based magic to complete an ancient spell  ; Celestia then proclaims Twilight ready for the next stage of her life, and Twilight transforms into an Alicorn and a Princess  .  As Princess Twilight Sparkle (the “Princess of Friendship”), she later gains her own crystal castle (the Castle of Friendship) and eventually – as of the series finale “The Last Problem” – becomes the ruler of Equestria, inheriting the throne from Celestia and Luna .

Twilight’s human counterpart in the Equestria Girls films, often called “Sci-Twi,” appears as well.  (Her human design wears black-framed glasses and ties her hair in a bun, with a wider pink streak than her pony version .)  Sci-Twi is depicted as an ultra‐logical high school student; she first meets pony-Twilight in the first Equestria Girls movie, and reappears in later films (notably Friendship Games where her corrupted alter-ego “Midnight Sparkle” is defeated)  .  Throughout all mediums, Twilight’s fundamental identity – love of learning, devotion to friends, and strong inner magic – remains consistent.

Personality and Traits

Twilight Sparkle’s defining traits are her intellect, organization, and dedication to learning  .  From childhood she “studied magic with great interest” and yearned to attend Princess Celestia’s School for Gifted Unicorns .  She is naturally introverted and highly studious: in the pilot she even tries to excuse herself from a friend gathering to go study in her room .  However, under Celestia’s guidance Twilight learns to open up; by the end of Season 1 she eagerly begs to stay in Ponyville with her new friends .  Over the series Twilight becomes warm-hearted and loyal to her friends – genuinely forgiving and supportive (even for reformed villains like Starlight Glimmer ).  She keeps in regular contact with Celestia via “friendship reports” (detailed letters on her experiences) , and she cares deeply about her duties and responsibilities.

Twilight has a love of books, knowledge, and structured routines.  For the first four seasons she lives in the Golden Oak Library in Ponyville, often shown reading or cataloging books .  She is described in-show as inquisitive and well-read – for example, after Trixie boasts of beating an Ursa Major, Twilight excitedly researches the creature in the library ; she also reads through the entire Daring Do adventure series for fun .  Twilight’s intelligence and magical talent are extraordinary: even as a filly Princess Celestia notes she has “never seen a unicorn with so much raw ability” .  Over the series she invents/discovers many spells: for cloud-walking (Sonic Rainboom ), for transforming objects (turning an apple into a carriage in The Best Night Ever ), time-travel (the two-sicles in It’s About Time ), dimension-shifting, and more.  Her greatest feat is completing Star Swirl’s unfinished spell in “Magical Mystery Cure,” the power of which turns her into an alicorn .  (In other episodes she safely teleports herself and others multiple times, shatters enchantments, and uses protective shields against pow
creator: MLPguy
